html:
The Vendetta Gazette

Vol. XII • May 25, 2026 • “All the Blood That’s Fit to Print” • Two Cents (If You’re Still Alive to Pay)

★ TERRITORY GAINS THIS WEEK ★ Donald Trump     +41
Sir Caprice       +13
Cokro             +13
Shannyn          +1
Wu-Tang          +1
Blood Captain    +1

The Annihilation of Wcrypto — A City Watches in Horror

Trump's Golden Juggernaut Claims Forty-One Turfs and Shatters a Rival's Entire Empire to Dust By Our Man in the Trenches, Ignatius P. Flambeau

This correspondent has, in the long and blood-soaked years of his tenure at this august publication, borne witness to conquests, collapses, and catastrophes of every conceivable variety. But what Donald Trump — the Platinum Pompadour, the Golden Emperor, the man whose ambition appears subject to no earthly ceiling — visited upon the unfortunate Wcrypto this past week was not merely a defeat. It was an obliteration. A vaporisation. The sort of comprehensive territorial dismemberment that leaves cartographers weeping into their inkwells.

Forty-one turfs, dear reader. Forty-one — precisely matching his extraordinary haul of the fortnight prior, as though the Emperor consulted some infernal ledger and decided that the number forty-one had a certain pleasing symmetry to it. Every single one of those blocks was peeled, one by one, from the shivering carcass of the Wcrypto organisation. The poor wretch lost his HQ on the twelfth of May — Trump's enforcers having stormed the gates while the week was still young and tender — and from that moment forward, Wcrypto was a dead man who hadn't yet had the decency to lie down. He lost four hundred and three garrison units in the carnage, leaving a skeletal force of four hundred and sixty-four rattling about in what remains of his holdings. The man has been raided seventy-one times and captured from forty-three. Seventy-one, reader. One shudders to imagine the state of his vaults.

And yet — as your correspondent noted with some bemusement last week — the Emperor's own army has not merely held steady. Trump added two hundred and sixty-four fresh soldiers to his payroll, swelling his garrison to a jaw-dropping five thousand two hundred and eighty units. The man conquers with one hand and recruits with the other. He also found time to conduct seventy-six raids across the city, including a particularly audacious sortie against Blood Captain that netted a clean five hundred dollars in cash, five hundred in arms, and nearly thirty XP — the single largest haul of the week. It appears the Emperor does not merely want your territory. He wants your furniture, your silverware, and the gold fillings from your teeth.

Sir Caprice Arrives — And Numiª Ceases to Exist

A Dashing New Contender Storms an HQ, Builds the City's Mightiest Army, and Announces Himself to Vendetta City in No Uncertain Terms By Ignatius P. Flambeau

If the Emperor's rampage dominated the early week, the closing act belonged to an altogether different class of scoundrel. Sir Caprice — and what a name, what a magnificent, swaggering, self-satisfied name — has materialised in this city's power structure like a well-dressed thunderclap. Thirteen turfs seized. Two hundred and seventy-nine garrison units added, bringing his total army to a staggering five thousand seven hundred and seventy-nine — the largest single garrison in all of Vendetta City, a fact that this correspondent suspects will not remain uncontested for long.

On the eighteenth of May, Sir Caprice delivered his calling card to one Numiª in the form of a full HQ capture — swift, clinical, and utterly terminal. Numiª, who had arrived in this city's ledgers only recently and possessed but a single turf to his name, had nowhere left to retreat. He has vanished from the rolls entirely. Whether he fled, fell, or simply thought better of the whole enterprise, this newspaper cannot say. What we can say is that Sir Caprice wasted no sentiment on the occasion.

Watch this one, reader. A man who builds an army of nearly six thousand and captures an HQ in the same week is not here to
